title: mentha × dentata var. agraria
intro: Mentha × dentata var. agraria, also known as Corn Mint or Garden Corn Mint, is a hybrid variety of the peppermint plant, derived from the cross between watermint (Mentha aquatica) and spearmint (Mentha spicata). It is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which make it useful in treating digestive issues, nausea, and headaches. The health benefits of Corn Mint include relief from indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its ability to ease respiratory issues and promote a good night's sleep. Corn Mint can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the skin for its cooling and soothing effects.
